rawText: On 15 July 2026 the ASA upheld a complaint against Phlo Technologies Ltd t/a Morrisons Clinic and Wm Morrison Supermarkets Ltd t/a Morrisons, ruling that a paid-for Facebook ad seen on 2 February 2026 promoted prescription-only medicines to the public in breach of CAP Code rule 12.12. The ad stated 'Morrisons Clinic weight loss service. From £79 a month, get access to our UK team of experts for monthly check-ins' and carried an image reading 'Why pay more for weight loss? Morrisons Clinic Together with phlo £79. Market average weight loss service £103'. The ASA found the ad clicked through a filtering page to landing pages referencing 'At-home, self-injectable pen designed to aid weight loss when injected once-weekly' and an order confirmation reading 'Treatment: Mounjaro KwikPen 5mg. 1 pre-filled disposable injection supply', and a page comparing Mounjaro (tirzepatide) with Wegovy (semaglutide) — both POMs. The ASA sought advice from the MHRA, which expressed concern the ad was likely to lead to the use of a POM. The ruling forms part of a wider piece of work on POMs used for weight loss, with related rulings published on 9 July 2025, 17 December 2025, 11 February 2026, 18 February 2026, 8 April 2026, 27 May 2026 and 24 June 2026. Number of complaints: 1. · ANGLES: (1) Eight ruling dates in twelve months on the same theme — the public record on weight-loss POM advertising, no editorialising needed. (2) What a supermarket brand on a weight-loss clinic does to a buyer's sense of what is routine. (3) The £79 vs £103 comparison — price framing on a service whose product is a prescription medicine. · APP CONNECTION: check claim — paste the ad or landing-page claim and the app flags that prescription-only treatments cannot be advertised to the public, and separates the service claim from the medicine claim. · SECONDARY: https://www.asa.org.uk/advice-online/weight-control-prescription-only-medicines.html (CAP AdviceOnline, grade A) · SOURCE GRADE: A (regulator, full ruling fetched and verified 15 July 2026). · NOTE: adjacent to, not inside, the supplement lane — pairs with the Kind Patches ruling as the supplement-side mirror.
